items.title: Email and SMS spam filtering
items.description: Early and modern spam filters estimate P(spam|word presence) via naive Bayes: multiply per-word class-conditional probabilities. Fast training on large corpora made it a production staple before deep learning.

items.title: Document and sentiment classification
items.description: Topic labeling, language detection, and sentiment analysis use Multinomial NB: each token contributes a factor P(word|class), combined under conditional independence for scalable text baselines.

items.title: Real-time triage with many binary sensors
items.description: IoT fault detection, fraud alerts, and safety filters with dozens of binary flags use naive Bayes because estimating pairwise feature interactions per class is infeasible at scale.

items.title: Medical symptom checkers (baseline)
items.description: Preliminary symptom-to-condition mapping can use naive Bayes when labeled symptom-outcome data is sparse—each symptom contributes an independent factor given the diagnosis, enabling quick probabilistic ranking.

items.title: Baseline benchmark in ML pipelines
items.description: Teams train Gaussian or Multinomial NB as a fast, interpretable baseline before gradient boosting or neural nets—validating that the independence-based generative model captures enough signal for the task.
